title: Podolobium ilicifolium
taxonomy: Podolobium ilicifolium is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and is commonly known as the holly-leaved podolobium or prickly-leaved podolobium. Within the genus Podolobium, P. ilicifolium is one of the several species found in Australia. The classification of P. ilicifolium is further supported by its botanical name, which is a combination of genus and species names. This plant is known for its unique holly-like leaves and its ability to thrive in dry environments.

intro: Podolobium ilicifolium, also known as the holly-leaved podolobium or prickly-leaved podolobium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian and Aboriginal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing the severity of respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Podolobium ilicifolium include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations.
